videos.title: New to Major League Pickleball? Watch this first.
videos.description:
It’s not just pickleball, it’s team pickleball.
Coed lineups. City pride. Matches that come down to a final DreamBreaker™.

This is Major League Pickleball, where team chemistry is the difference maker and every event builds toward the hardest trophy to win in the sport. 

20 teams. The 2026 season takes place over 13 events this summer. Join us at an event or tune-in and catch your favorite team.

videos.title: 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers
videos.description:
Tune in starting at 10am ET as the 2026 MLP Draft presented by Skechers unfolds live. Dave Fleming and Will Daughton are breaking down the first 28 picks as the first two male and two female roster slots are filled for each team. Expect real-time reactions, insights, and a front row seat to understanding teams' selections.
⁠
The remaining 38 selections and complete rosters will be posted in real-t

videos.description:

videos.title: Oceanview Named Title Sponsor of the 2026 Oceanview USA Pickleball National Championships
videos.description:
USA Pickleball is proud to announce Oceanview Life and Annuity as the new title sponsor of its premier event, now officially named the 2026 Oceanview USA Pickleball National Championships. The partnership marks a significant milestone for the organization and reinforces the continued momentum of pickleball as one of the fastest-growing sports in the country. 

Set to take place October 31–November

videos.title: What is Pickleball?
videos.description:
Pickleball is one of the fastest growing sports in the United States and for good reason. In this video, we break down what Pickleball is, why it’s so popular, and how you can get started playing.

Pickleball is a paddle sport that combines elements of tennis, table tennis, and badminton into one fun and accessible game. One of its biggest appeals is that players of all ages and skill levels can p

videos.title: Pickleball Fundamentals For Beginners
videos.description:
Having a strong foundation in pickleball fundamentals will set you up for long-term success on the court. In this video, we break down some of the most important fundamentals every beginner should learn to start playing with confidence.

First, it all starts with your grip. We recommend the continental grip, which you can find by forming a “V” with your dominant hand and placing it along the edge

videos.title: Most Important Rules to Start Playing Pickleball
videos.description:
If you don’t know the rules, you can’t play! In this video, we break down the most important pickleball rules so you can step on the court with confidence and start playing right away.

First, understand the boundaries of the court. The baselines and sidelines mark the limits, and for a ball to be in, it must land on or within these lines. If any part of the ball touches the line, it’s considered

items.title: Why Backhand-Ready Beats Forehand-First at the Kitchen
items.summary: Intermediate players should favor a slightly backhand-ready position at the kitchen because many speedups target the body, paddle shoulder, or middle. The backhand counter is compact, stable, and faster from crowded contact points—but use the forehand when the ball is clearly out
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Hit a Third Shot Drop That Stays Below the Net
items.summary: To hit a better third shot drop, don’t just aim for the kitchen. Shape the ball with a small arc so it peaks on your side, starts falling by the net, and reaches your opponent below knee height. A low, slightly deeper drop is usually safer than a high, short floater. Collin Johns
items.author: Ana

items.title: HEAD Radical PRO EX15 Pickleball Paddle Review
items.summary: Pickleball Union earns a commission on purchases made through links in this article, at no extra cost to you. Our reviews are editorially independent – brands do not see or approve content before publication. The HEAD Radical PRO EX15 is best for advanced beginners to intermediat
items.author: Drew McCartt

items.title: Watch Rachel Rohrabacher Rip a 2,273 RPM Spin Serve in Extreme Slow-Mo
items.summary: John Kew breaks down Rachel Rohrabacher's on-court technique using high-speed cameras and radar data. See what makes her game elite.
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: Learn the One Shot Top Pros Practice Most: the Reset
items.summary: It is much better to hit the world’s highest, worst reset that still makes it over the net and forces your opponent to put the ball away than it is to miss the ball in the net.
items.author: Eric Roddy

items.title: Master the Forehand Speed-Up: Fix These Common Mistakes
items.summary: Your forehand speed-up doesn't have to sail long or crash into the net. A forehand speed-up requires precise mechanics, body positioning, and timing to become a reliable weapon in your game.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: These 6 Pickleball Shots Are Shaping the Future of the Game in 2026
items.summary: The pickleball shot landscape is evolving fast, and the players who master these six techniques will have a serious competitive advantage. From the two-handed backhand to the off-the-bounce combo, here's what you need to know to stay ahead in 2026.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The 4 Rules of a Successful Attack in Pickleball (Infographic)
items.summary: Most intermediate pickleball players have the same shots as advanced players but don't know when to use them. The biggest culprit? A flawed pickleball attack strategy that costs you points.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 5 Pickleball Skills That Make You Unattackable
items.summary: Professional pickleball players have mastered specific pickleball skills that make them nearly impossible to attack. Ashley Griffith breaks down the exact techniques high-level players use to stay confident and stable during rallies.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: [SEO - Alex] 5 Essential Senior Pickleball Strategies Every Player Should Master
items.summary: Senior pickleball strategies aren't about playing less, they're about playing smarter. Here are five essential tactics that help older players win more points, stay injury-free, and outlast opponents who rely on pure power.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Infographic: 6 Strategies to Beat Bangers in Pickleball
items.summary: Hard hitters can feel overwhelming, but learning how to beat bangers in pickleball doesn't require matching their power. Pro coach Michael Loyd breaks down six control tactics that neutralize aggressive players and put you back in charge of the point.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Cover a Pickleball Lob: The 'Open the Door' Footwork That Keeps You Safe
items.summary: Covering a pickleball lob doesn't have to be dangerous. Learn the "open the door" footwork technique that keeps you safe while improving your game, plus four other essential tips for 50+ recreational players.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Pickleball Kitchen Strategy for 3.5 Players: What to Focus on First
items.summary: Kitchen strategy 3.5 pickleball players need is less complicated than you think, the gap between a 3.0 and a 3.5 comes down to a handful of repeatable habits at the non-volley zone. Here's exactly where to focus first so you stop losing points you should be winning.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Speed Up From the Kitchen in Pickleball: Timing & Target Guide for 3.5–4.0
items.summary: Knowing when to speed up from the kitchen in pickleball is the difference between earning a point and handing one away. This guide breaks down the exact timing triggers, target zones, and attack patterns 3.5–4.0 players need to start winning more exchanges at the net.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Who could challenge the top seeds next PPA Tour season?
items.summary: We take a closer look at some partnerships that might be able to challenge the top seeds when the PPA Tour begins its 2026-27 season in the fall. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: How to Manage Tournament Nerves in Pickleball: Pre-Match Mental Prep
items.summary: Pickleball tournament nerves hit almost every competitive player, even the ones who look ice-cold on court. This guide breaks down exactly how to manage pre-match anxiety with mental prep strategies that work when the pressure is on.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Hit a Penetrating Third Shot Drop in Pickleball
items.summary: The third shot drop is one of pickleball's most essential shots, and mastering an aggressive version can transform your game. Here are the three keys that separate casual players from competitive ones.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 5 Pickleball IQ Moves That Will Transform Your Match Strategy
items.summary: Coach Jess breaks down five pickleball IQ moves designed to give you a strategic edge over your opponents. These aren't just technical skills—they're the mental shifts that separate winning players from the rest.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Skinny Singles Pickleball: The Drill That Changes Your Game
items.summary: Skinny singles pickleball is one of the most effective two-player drills for improving your doubles game. This focused practice method forces accuracy and builds the decision-making skills that separate competitive players from casual ones.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How 11SIX24 went from an under-the-radar brand to major player in the paddle market
items.summary: David Groechel started 11SIX24 in a small apartment in Washington, D.C., in 2023. Since then the brand has grown into a key player in the pickleball paddle market. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: Stop Getting Jammed at the Kitchen With This Contact Point Fix
items.summary: A better pickleball contact point starts before the ball arrives. Keep your paddle spaced slightly away from your body, elbows forward, wrist stable, and chest engaged. This gives you room to counter, block, or reset instead of getting jammed when opponents speed the ball up at t
items.author: Ana

items.title: Burned Out on Pickleball? Why It Happens — and How to Get the Spark Back
items.summary: Pickleball burnout is common when you play too often, chase ratings, repeat the same games, or turn the sport into pressure instead of fun. To recover, take a short break, reduce volume, change groups, drill instead of playing, add recovery days, and return with a lighter, more i
items.author: Ana

items.title: Selkirk Sport announces acquisition of Bread & Butter Pickleball Company
items.summary: Selkirk Sport has announced the acquisition of Bread & Butter Pickleball Company, a smaller niche brand that has been well known in the paddle enthusiast community for several years. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: The 5-Second Reset Every Frustrated Pickleball Player Needs
items.summary: Getting angry at yourself in pickleball is common, but constant yelling or harsh self-talk can make you tighter, distract your partner, and lead to more mistakes. Use a quick reset: name the error, choose one correction, breathe, reconnect with your partner, and move on to the ne
items.author: Ana

items.title: 7 Reliable Ways to Know When to Let the Ball Go Out in Pickleball
items.summary: Let the ball go out in pickleball when it is shoulder-high or rising, hit from below net height, struck with a big backswing, or coming from an off-balance opponent. Also watch court position, spin, angle, and wind. The goal is to stop saving your opponent’s bad attacks. One of t
items.author: Ana

items.title: 2026 PPA Tour Finals recap: Ben Johns, Anna Leigh Waters complete near perfect spring seasons
items.summary: Anna Leigh Waters and Ben Johns put the exclamation point on an incredible run this spring with clean sweeps in the doubles divisions on Sunday at the PPA Tour Finals. More
items.author: The Kitchen

items.title: How Long After Surgery Before You Can Play Pickleball Again?
items.summary: Most rec players can return to pickleball after surgery, but timelines vary. Knee or hip replacements often take 3–6 months for controlled doubles, while meniscus repairs, rotator cuff repairs, and ACL surgery may take longer. Return should depend on pain, swelling, strength, bal
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Counter Like a 5.0 in Pickleball
items.summary: To counter in pickleball like a higher-level player, keep your paddle out front, use a stable wrist, stay balanced, and cover likely attack zones. Counter balls into your prepared paddle or chest-height zone, but reset or let go balls that are low, late, outside your frame, or sa
items.author: Ana

items.title: The $225 Million Pickleball Deal Nobody Explained to the 24 Million People Who Actually Play
items.summary: You probably saw the headline last week. Pickleball Inc. raised $225 million. “A seismic day,” said Connor Pardoe, the PPA Tour founder who now runs the whole operation. CNBC covered it. CBS covered it. Yahoo Sports covered it. What none of them covered is what the deal actually
items.author: Paul Lemley

items.summary: We wanted to learn more about Tama Shimabukuro, so earlier this week we invited him on PBallers, our remote version of Pickleballers. More

items.summary: Fundraisers and meal trains have been set up for the families of the five people who were killed in a Texas plane crash on their way to a pickleball tournament last week. More

items.summary: Pickleball Inc. has become the new parent company of the PPA Tour and Major League Pickleball following a $225 million investment led by Apollo Global Management’s sports fund, Apollo Sports Capital. More
